# Contributing to awhile

First off, thank you for considering contributing to awhile! It's people like you that make awhile a great tool for everyone.

## Code of Conduct

By participating in this project, you are expected to uphold our Code of Conduct. Please be respectful and professional in all interactions.

## How Can I Contribute?

### Reporting Bugs

* Check if the bug has already been reported in the Issues section.
* If not, create a new issue. Include as much detail as possible, such as steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and actual behavior.
* Screenshots are always helpful!

### Suggesting Enhancements

* Enhancement suggestions are tracked as GitHub issues.
* Before creating a new suggestion, check if it has already been proposed.
* Provide a clear and concise description of the proposed feature and why it would be useful.

### Pull Requests

1. Fork the repository.
2. Create a new branch for your feature or bug fix: `git checkout -b feature/my-new-feature`.
3. Make your changes and commit them with descriptive commit messages.
4. Ensure your code follows the existing style and all tests pass (if any).
5. Push your branch to your fork: `git push origin feature/my-new-feature`.
6. Open a Pull Request against the `main` branch of the original repository.

## Development Setup

See the [README](../README.md#development) for instructions on how to set up the project locally.

## License

By contributing to awhile, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the [AGPL-3.0 License](../LICENSE).
